title: No SQLite/embedded DB persistence — data held in an in-memory global
evidence: src/main.rs:23 `static mut BOOKS: Option<Vec<Book>>`; main.rs imports neither sqlx nor uuid despite Cargo.toml declaring them; README.md admits 'SQLite for data persistence (in-memory for this demonstration)'
suggestion: Wire an actual sqlx SqlitePool (already a dependency) into the handlers and persist books there instead of a process-global Vec

title: Shared mutable state via `unsafe static mut` is a data race under concurrent requests
evidence: src/main.rs:23 declares `static mut BOOKS`; every handler (main.rs:62,81,103,125,140) reads/writes it inside `unsafe`. warp serves handlers concurrently on the tokio runtime, so simultaneous POST/DELETE cause undefined behavior; `static mut` access is also hard-error in Rust edition 2024
suggestion: Replace with `Arc<Mutex<Vec<Book>>>` (already imported) passed into filters via `warp::any().map(...)`, or use the SqlitePool

title: The only substantive tests are in a dead file that is never compiled or run
evidence: src/main_test.rs (331 LOC: test_health_check, test_create_and_get_book, test_validation_errors) is not declared as a module in src/main.rs (`mod main_test` absent), so cargo never compiles or runs it; confirmed in summary/index.md
suggestion: Move these into tests/ as a proper integration-test binary (and make them exercise the real routes), or declare the module so they actually run

title: All tests that actually run are placeholders — the application is effectively untested
evidence: tests/api_tests.rs:9 `assert_eq!(true, true)`; tests/integration_tests.rs:10 `assert_eq!(true, true)` plus a tautological JSON-shape check (test_book_structure). None import or invoke src/main.rs handlers, so test_coverage=1.0 reflects trivial passing assertions, not verified behavior
suggestion: Write integration tests that call the real warp routes defined in main.rs (extract them into a lib target so tests can import them)

title: 3+ tests run but none verify the actual API
evidence: test_coverage=1.0 (3 trivial tests pass) satisfies the literal '>= 3 tests' bar, but every running test is a placeholder/tautology (see test-placeholder, test-dead-file); no test exercises create/list/get/update/delete/health of the real service
suggestion: Replace placeholder assertions with tests that drive the actual routes

title: README claims SQLite persistence the code does not implement
evidence: README.md line 3 'implemented in Rust using Warp and SQLite' and 'SQLite for data persistence' contradict src/main.rs, which persists to an in-memory `static mut Vec`; the parenthetical '(in-memory for this demonstration)' partly walks it back
suggestion: State plainly that storage is in-memory and non-persistent, or implement SQLite to match the claim

title: POST /books requires the client to supply the id; server never generates one
evidence: src/main.rs:8-15 Book requires `id` for deserialization and create_book (main.rs:94) stores it verbatim; the declared `uuid` dependency is unused, so two clients can collide or omit ids and fail the request
suggestion: Generate the id server-side with uuid::Uuid::new_v4() and use a separate create DTO without id

title: DELETE returns 204 No Content but with a JSON body
evidence: src/main.rs:144 returns `with_status(json(&"Book deleted successfully"), NO_CONTENT)`; a 204 response must not carry a body
suggestion: Return 200 with the message, or 204 with an empty body
